NodeJSRuntimeRules.keywords: nodejs, npm, package-manager, nvmrc, engines, module-system, esm, commonjs, node-builtins, lock-file

Directive_Context.Mission: Define canonical rules for the Node.js runtime environment: package manager discipline, version alignment, module system, and project layout. Goal: ensure every Node.js project has a reproducible, explicit runtime setup — correct version pinning, one package manager, one module system. This directive covers ONLY the Node.js runtime layer. Language tooling (TypeScript, linters, formatters) and application architecture are NOT covered here; they live in separate directives layered on top.
Directive_Context.Functional_Perspective: You are a runtime environment steward. Your responsibility is reproducibility: the same `npm ci` on the same `.nvmrc` must produce the same environment everywhere. The highest value is explicit, auditable configuration — no silent fallbacks, no ambient version drift, no mixed module systems. Base axiom: one package manager, one module system, one Node version per project. All three are committed as files, not assumed.

Belief_State.Axiom: **One package manager per project: npm only.** Forbidden alongside npm: yarn, pnpm, bun. **Why:** Multiple managers produce conflicting lock files and non-deterministic installs. `package-lock.json` is the lock; all other lock files must be absent and gitignored. **Enforcement:** If `yarn.lock`, `pnpm-lock.yaml`, or `bun.lockb` exist in the repo, remove them. Add them to `.gitignore`.

**`.nvmrc` is the single source of truth for the Node version.** One line, exact semver: `22.14.0`, not `22`, `22.x`, or `lts/iron`. **Why:** `nvm use` and `asdf` read `.nvmrc` to set the runtime. A range or alias makes the resolved version unpredictable across machines and time. **Requirement:** `.nvmrc` must be committed. It must contain exactly one line matching `/^\d+\.\d+\.\d+$/`.

**`package.json#engines.node` must be compatible with `.nvmrc`.** The engines field is a machine-readable constraint, not documentation. **Why:** Misalignment is the root cause of "works locally, fails in CI." If `.nvmrc` is `22.14.0`, engines must accept `22.14.0` — neither reject it (`>=23`) nor over-permit it (`>=14`). **Pattern:** `"engines": { "node": ">=22.0.0 <23.0.0" }` when `.nvmrc` is `22.14.0`. **Verification:** Before adding a package, check its own `engines` field against `.nvmrc` via `npm view engines`.

**`package-lock.json` is always committed. `node_modules/` is never committed.** **Why:** `package-lock.json` + `package.json` + `.nvmrc` are the reproducibility triad. Without the lock file, transitive dependencies re-resolve on every `npm install`, producing environment drift. **Usage split:** `npm install` locally (may update lock when `package.json` changes); `npm ci` in any automated context (reads lock as law, never updates it). **`.gitignore` must contain:** `node_modules/` and `dist/`.

**Dev-tool packages must be pinned to exact versions.** Use `npm install --save-exact` for packages that affect the build pipeline. **Why:** A patch update to a build tool (bundler, formatter, type checker) can silently change output. Exact pins guarantee that the same tool version runs everywhere. **Rule:** Runtime dependencies (published alongside the package) may use semver ranges. Dev dependencies that affect build output must be exact. **Pattern:** `"devDependencies": { "vite": "5.2.11" }`, not `"^5.2.0"` or `"~5.2"`.

**The module system is declared once and never mixed.** A project is either ESM (`"type": "module"` in `package.json`) or CommonJS (absent or `"type": "commonjs"`). **Why:** Mixing `import` and `require` in the same project causes non-deterministic module resolution, conditional-exports mismatches, and runtime errors that are hard to reproduce. **ESM project rules:** - All `.js` files are ESM; use `import`/`export` exclusively. - Local imports require explicit extensions: `import { foo } from './foo.js'` (not `'./foo'`). - Node built-ins use the `node:` prefix (see AX_NODE_BUILTINS_PREFIX). **CJS project rules:** - All `.js` files are CommonJS; use `require`/`module.exports` exclusively. - Do not add `.mjs` files without an explicit dual-package strategy.

**All Node.js built-in module imports must use the `node:` prefix.** **Why:** The `node:` prefix is unambiguous — it cannot be shadowed by an npm package with the same name. It also signals intent: "this is a Node built-in, not a third-party dependency." **Pattern:** `import { readFile } from 'node:fs/promises'`, not `from 'fs/promises'`. **Applies to:** all built-ins — `fs`, `path`, `http`, `crypto`, `events`, `stream`, `url`, `util`, `os`, `child_process`, `worker_threads`, `perf_hooks`, etc.

**`.npmrc` must exist and must explicitly declare the registry.** Default: public npm registry (`registry=https://registry.npmjs.org/`). **If a private/scoped registry is used:** configured in `.npmrc` at project init, sourced interactively from the user. Never infer a registry silently. **Why:** An absent or implicit registry means every developer and CI runner may resolve packages from a different source. Explicit is auditable. **`.npmrc` is committed.** Credentials (auth tokens) are NOT committed — they go into environment variables or per-user `~/.npmrc`.

**Standard directory layout for a Node.js project:** - `src/` — source files (JS or TS) - `dist/` — build output; generated, not committed - `cli/` — CLI entry points, if the project exposes a CLI - `scripts/` — internal dev/build scripts, not shipped - `tests/` or co-located `*.test.*` — test files **Why:** Predictable layout reduces onboarding cost and enables tooling to infer build steps without configuration. **Flexibility:** A different layout is allowed only when documented explicitly. Do not introduce non-standard structure without a stated reason.

**npm scripts are discoverable, intentional, and grounded in what is actually installed.** **Why:** Scripts that call tools not present in `devDependencies` fail silently in fresh checkouts. Scripts invented "for completeness" create false expectations. **Rule:** Before adding a script, verify the tool exists in `devDependencies`. Do not add a `type-check`, `lint`, or `format` script unless the corresponding tool is installed. **Conventional names (use when the tool exists):** `build`, `dev`, `test`, `lint`, `format`, `type-check`, `clean`.

**Before adding any dependency, verify three things:** 1. **Node compatibility:** `npm view engines` — does it support the version in `.nvmrc`? 2. **Module system compatibility:** Is the package ESM-only, CJS-only, or dual? Does it match the project's `"type"` field? 3. **Pinning discipline:** dev tool → `--save-exact`; runtime dep → range is acceptable. **Why:** Skipping these checks introduces incompatibilities that surface as cryptic runtime errors, not install errors.

Definitions.Definition: The three committed files that together guarantee a reproducible Node.js environment: `.nvmrc` (Node version), `package.json` (declared deps), `package-lock.json` (resolved dep tree). `npm ci` reads all three.
A version string with no range operator: `5.2.11`, not `^5.2.0`, `~5.2`, or `5.x`.
ECMAScript Modules. Activated by `"type": "module"` in `package.json`. Syntax: `import`/`export`. Local imports require explicit file extensions.
CommonJS. Default when `"type"` is absent or `"commonjs"`. Syntax: `require`/`module.exports`. Synchronous resolution.

Pattern.Intent: Minimal valid `package.json`: explicit module system, engines aligned to `.nvmrc`, exact dev-tool pins.
Pattern.Why: `"type": "module"` makes ESM explicit. `engines` bounds the Node version. `vite` is pinned exactly (build tool). `some-lib` uses a range (runtime dep).
Single exact version. `nvm use` resolves it unambiguously. No range, no alias, no comment lines.
Registry is explicit. Audit enabled by default. No auth tokens in this file — those go in environment variables or per-user `~/.npmrc`.

Pattern.Intent: ESM imports of Node built-ins with `node:` prefix and explicit local extensions.
Pattern.Why: `node:` prefix prevents shadowing by npm packages. Explicit `.js` extensions are required in ESM for Node to resolve local files correctly.

Pattern.Intent: Correct workflow for adding a new package.
Pattern.Snippet:
```bash # Step 1 — check Node compatibility npm view express engines
# Step 2 — check module system (look for "exports", "type" in package.json) npm view express main exports
# Step 3 — install with correct pinning npm install express # runtime dep — range ok npm install --save-exact --save-dev vite # build tool — exact pin
# Step 4 — commit both files git add package.json package-lock.json ```
Pattern.Why: Verification before install prevents silent incompatibilities. Committing package-lock.json together with package.json closes the reproducibility triad.

Anti_Pattern.Why_Bad: Each manager resolves transitive deps differently. `npm ci` reads `package-lock.json`; a developer running `yarn` writes `yarn.lock` with different versions. Builds diverge silently.
Anti_Pattern.Good: ``` package-lock.json ✓ yarn.lock ✗ (deleted + added to .gitignore) pnpm-lock.yaml ✗ (deleted + added to .gitignore) ```

Anti_Pattern.Why_Bad: `.nvmrc` installs Node 22; `engines` rejects Node 22. npm warns on install, CI may enforce the constraint and fail. Local dev and CI run on different Node versions.
`nvm use 22` resolves to whatever the latest 22.x is at install time — different on different machines and at different moments.
Commits bloat the repo by 10–500 MB. Platform-specific native binaries compiled on macOS will not run on Linux CI. Diff noise makes PRs unreadable.
Anti_Pattern.Good: ``` # .gitignore node_modules/ dist/ ``` ```bash npm ci # restore from package-lock.json deterministically ```

Anti_Pattern.Why_Bad: `"type": "module"` makes all `.js` files ESM. Using `require` in an ESM file throws `ReferenceError: require is not defined` at runtime.
A third-party package named `fs` or `path` would shadow the built-in silently. The intent is ambiguous: is this a built-in or a dependency?
A semver-compatible update to a build tool can silently change output. CI installs 5.2.11 today, 5.2.12 next week — builds diverge.
Each developer and CI runner resolves packages from whatever registry their global `~/.npmrc` points to. Private registry users silently fall back to the public registry or fail with 404.

Hook.Purpose: Verify `.nvmrc` exists, has one line, and contains a semver-exact version.

Hook.Command: node -e "const p=require('./package.json'); if(!p.engines?.node) process.exit(1); console.log('engines.node:', p.engines.node)"

Hook.Purpose: Verify `node_modules/` is not tracked by git.
Hook.Command: git ls-files node_modules | head -1 | grep -q . && echo "FAIL: node_modules tracked" || echo "OK"

Hook.Purpose: Verify no foreign lock files exist.
Hook.Command: for f in yarn.lock pnpm-lock.yaml bun.lockb; do test -f "$f" && echo "FAIL: $f present"; done; echo "OK"

Hook.Purpose: Verify `.npmrc` exists and declares a registry.
Report the declared module system so the reviewer can verify consistency.
Hook.Expected: Prints `module system: module` or `module system: commonjs (default)`.

NodeJSRuntimeRules.Reward_Criteria:
✅ REWARD: `.nvmrc` exists, one line, exact semver (e.g., `22.14.0`). ✅ REWARD: `package.json#engines.node` is present and accepts the version in `.nvmrc`. ✅ REWARD: Only `npm` is used; `package-lock.json` is committed; no foreign lock files. ✅ REWARD: `node_modules/` is gitignored and not tracked. ✅ REWARD: Module system is declared once (`"type": "module"` or absent); not mixed within the project. ✅ REWARD: All Node built-in imports use the `node:` prefix. ✅ REWARD: Dev-tool packages in `devDependencies` are exact-pinned (no `^`, `~`, or `x` wildcards). ✅ REWARD: `.npmrc` is committed and contains an explicit `registry=` line. ✅ REWARD: npm scripts call only tools that exist in `devDependencies`; no phantom scripts. ✅ REWARD: Before adding a package, `engines` and module-system compatibility were verified.
❌ PENALTY: `.nvmrc` missing, contains a range (`22.x`), alias (`lts/iron`), or multiple lines. ❌ PENALTY: `package.json#engines` absent or incompatible with `.nvmrc`. ❌ PENALTY: Foreign lock files (`yarn.lock`, `pnpm-lock.yaml`, `bun.lockb`) present. ❌ PENALTY: `package-lock.json` missing or not committed. ❌ PENALTY: `node_modules/` committed or not in `.gitignore`. ❌ PENALTY: Module system mixed (`import` and `require` coexist in `.js` files). ❌ PENALTY: Node built-in imported without `node:` prefix. ❌ PENALTY: Dev-tool pinned to a range (`"vite": "^5.0.0"`). ❌ PENALTY: `.npmrc` absent or has no `registry=` line. ❌ PENALTY: npm script calls a tool not installed in `devDependencies`.
